claim_statement: The current paper claims executable domain-level lifted AgentSpeak(L) library synthesis for positive conjunctive achievement-goal PDDL problem classes Q_D only when Q_D admits a goal-conditioned, feature-definable, terminating modular policy sketch with bounded sketch width, the selected feature rules have safe lifted bindings to declared PDDL predicates, primitive actions, predicate subgoals, read-only goal descriptors, or read-only agenda gates, and the compiled library validates without runtime full-trace planning.
selection_principle: Benchmark classes are selected by prior formal properties and materialized from complete IPC PDDL directories in potassco/pddl-instances at commit cf19edf7c53d1540ddbb396c642595e0926ee552. A domain is counted only after its local snapshot has a domain.pddl file, all official instances from the selected IPC directory, a deterministic floor(2/3 * N) training split, and a remaining held-out goal-specification split.

prior_benchmark_practice.property_axis: goal independence and Easy-to-Solve Hard-to-Optimise structure
prior_benchmark_practice.selection_style: The paper first defines TGI, SGI, and OGI, then evaluates domains expected to fit or stress those assumptions. Rovers and Transport remove path-finding components for the main goal-regression setting.
prior_benchmark_practice.implication_for_this_paper: Use goal-independent domains as the goal-separable and serialisable selected class and as MOOSE-style baselines. Do not treat them as covered unless they pass the same compiled-ASL protocol.

prior_benchmark_practice.property_axis: learned well-formed sketches with bounded sketch width
prior_benchmark_practice.selection_style: The paper chooses tractable IPC-style classes where small training instances can be fully explored and a learned sketch can be checked by SIWR on larger held-out instances.
prior_benchmark_practice.implication_for_this_paper: Use bounded sketch width as the main property basis. A credible broad evaluation should include more than one standard sketchable family.

prior_benchmark_practice.property_axis: handcrafted sketches, subgoal structure, and bounded effective width
prior_benchmark_practice.selection_style: The papers analyze problem classes Q_D, prove or test sketch well-formedness and small width, and use standard benchmark suites where the sketch property is expected to matter.
prior_benchmark_practice.implication_for_this_paper: State support over problem classes Q_D and explicit properties, not over arbitrary domains. Use unsupported route or numeric fragments as boundary rows.

prior_benchmark_practice.property_axis: feature-definable terminating general policies learned from small examples
prior_benchmark_practice.selection_style: The paper evaluates problem classes where a finite description-logic feature pool can separate good transitions and defines a terminating policy, including full Blocksworld with goal-dependent construction.
prior_benchmark_practice.implication_for_this_paper: Blocksworld is the right core goal-dependency domain, but one standard family is narrower than this precedent.

prior_benchmark_practice.property_axis: goal-conditioned lifted decision-list policies executed directly on held-out problems
prior_benchmark_practice.selection_style: The paper evaluates policy execution on held-out problems with more objects than training, across domains suited to the lifted decision-list language.
prior_benchmark_practice.implication_for_this_paper: A broad executable-policy claim should include several standard domains; Ferry, Gripper, Miconic, and Spanner are natural comparators.

prior_benchmark_practice.property_axis: terminating generalized programs with loops and validation on large instances
prior_benchmark_practice.selection_style: The paper groups domains by whether compact loop programs can solve all input instances and then validates the synthesized programs on larger instances.
prior_benchmark_practice.implication_for_this_paper: Program-synthesis papers support larger benchmark breadth; our narrower artifact-specific claim must be explicit unless we add more compiled-ASL domains.

domain_count_assessment.prior_broad_gp_domain_count_range: 6-13 standard domains for broad generalized-planning method papers, with MOOSE additionally probing 38 IPC domains for goal-independence behavior.

required_families: goal-separable or serialisable achievement classes: gripper, miconic, and logistics
bounded-width sketchable subgoal-structure classes: barman, childsnack, and visitall
feature-definable goal-dependent construction classes: blocks and depots

domain_count_assessment.current_paper_positioning: The benchmark infrastructure now targets eight achievement-goal IPC domains materialized from complete official IPC directories. Strict ASL-library coverage remains an evaluation result to be established per split, but benchmark selection no longer depends on generated problem snapshots.

goal_specification_layers.name: Positive conjunctive achievement-goal layer
goal_specification_layers.definition: The problem objective is a final-state PDDL goal formula in the positive conjunctive fragment. The learned output is a domain-level lifted AgentSpeak(L) library whose heads are declared PDDL predicate achievement goals and whose top-level composer is +!g.
goal_specification_layers.pddl_relation: Ordinary classical PDDL problem goals define this layer directly when they are positive conjunctive state formulas.
goal_specification_layers.paper_use: All three selected domain classes are first evaluated in this layer.

goal_specification_layers.name: LTLf temporal-extended-goal layer
goal_specification_layers.definition: The temporal objective is stored as an LTLf formula, compiled to a deterministic finite automaton, and executed above the same achievement-goal library. The DFA chooses the next fluent condition or conjunction to realize; it is query-specific while the achievement-goal library remains domain-level.
goal_specification_layers.pddl_relation: Classical PDDL :goal does not define arbitrary TEGs. PDDL3 trajectory constraints can encode some temporal constraints, but the project representation remains LTLf-to-DFA for the full temporal layer.
goal_specification_layers.paper_use: This is a goal-specification layer, not a fourth domain class. Later evaluation should overlay TEGs on representative domains from each selected domain class.

formal_properties.name: Goal independence and goal-regression suitability
formal_properties.definition: MOOSE defines True Goal Independence, Serialisable Goal Independence, and Optimal Goal Independence over conjunctive goals. These properties justify greedy singleton-goal regression when the goal atoms can be achieved independently or in a valid serial order.
formal_properties.paper_use: Selected-class and baseline property. It explains why Ferry, Gripper, Miconic, and Logistics belong in the goal-separable and serialisable class, and why Blocksworld-style goal dependence should not be treated as MOOSE-friendly.

formal_properties.name: Well-formed terminating policy sketches with bounded sketch width
formal_properties.definition: Sketch papers choose domains where a policy sketch over qualitative features is well-formed or acyclic and yields bounded-width subproblems for SIWR-style search.
formal_properties.paper_use: Primary theoretical inclusion property for our hypothesis class. Our ASL output replaces planner-guided subproblem solving with compiled predicate-goal modules.

formal_properties.name: Feature-definable general policies
formal_properties.definition: A class Q_D is suitable when a finite feature pool over the PDDL predicates contains features that separate good and bad transitions and define a terminating general policy for all instances in the class.
formal_properties.paper_use: Main construction-class justification. Prior work explicitly learns feature policies for clear(x), on(x,y), and full Blocksworld using features such as blocks above a target and well-placed blocks; Depots is selected as an IPC construction benchmark that combines transport-style location goals with Blocksworld-style crate stacking.

formal_properties.name: Executable generalized-policy hypothesis classes
formal_properties.definition: PG3 evaluates lifted decision-list policies over PDDL domains, while BFGP++ synthesizes terminating C++-style generalized programs with loops.
formal_properties.paper_use: Related executable baselines. They motivate our executable-output requirement but do not target predicate-goal AgentSpeak(L) modules.

formal_properties.name: Safe lifted AgentSpeak(L) compilability
formal_properties.definition: Every selected rule must compile to lifted ASL using only declared PDDL primitive actions, declared predicate achievement subgoals, read-only goal descriptors, or read-only agenda gates. Object-specific features, undeclared vocabulary, unbound variables, and unsupported distance or numeric semantics are rejected.
formal_properties.paper_use: Our additional artifact-specific property. It is the reason a qualitative sketch or policy is evidence, not automatically an executable ASL library.

benchmark_classes.name: Goal-separable and serialisable achievement classes
formal_membership: goal atoms are true-goal-independent, serialisable-goal-independent, or weakly coupled in the MOOSE sense
single-goal progress can be composed without global construction-style goal dependency
compiled ASL validates without runtime full-trace planning
benchmark_classes.paper_role: Selected property-defined class for the final broad evaluation. These domains test whether compiled lifted ASL libraries cover the same goal-separable or serialisable region used by MOOSE, PG3, D2L, and sketch-learning papers.
current_evidence: Complete IPC Gripper, Miconic, and Logistics directories are materialized from potassco/pddl-instances with floor(2/3) train/test splits.

benchmark_classes.name: Bounded-width sketchable subgoal-structure classes
formal_membership: well-formed or acyclic sketches over qualitative features
bounded sketch width or bounded effective subproblem width in prior sketch literature
resource, one-way movement, or repeated-subgoal decisions may require progress-preserving choices
compiled ASL validates without runtime full-trace planning
benchmark_classes.paper_role: Selected property-defined class for the final broad evaluation. These domains test whether the ASL compiler can preserve bounded-width sketchable subgoal structure rather than only independent-goal dispatch.
current_evidence: Complete IPC Barman, Childsnack, and Visitall directories are materialized from potassco/pddl-instances with floor(2/3) train/test splits.

benchmark_classes.name: Feature-definable goal-dependent stacking and construction classes
formal_membership: final goals can have strong causal dependency and cannot be achieved in arbitrary order
a finite lifted feature set defines a terminating general policy or modular sketch
predicate-goal modules and a goal-conditioned composer can express construction order
compiled ASL validates without runtime full-trace planning
benchmark_classes.paper_role: Selected property-defined class for the final broad evaluation. This class captures the strongest goal-dependency argument for lifted ASL modules: construction goals where support, stacking, or carrier-resource structure constrains the order of atomic goals.
current_evidence: Complete IPC Blocks and Depots directories are materialized from potassco/pddl-instances with floor(2/3) train/test splits.
Blocks supplies the canonical feature-definable Blocksworld construction class; Depots supplies an IPC construction benchmark combining transport goals with Blocksworld-style crate stacking.

excluded_or_boundary_domains.reason: These domains require route planning, ranking, transitive closure, or multi-carrier location reasoning beyond the current safe lifted feature-binding contract. MOOSE also removes path-finding components from Rovers and Transport for its main goal-regression setting.
Instrument calibration and resource coupling introduce dependencies that are not yet part of the selected three-class evaluation target.
The current paper scope is positive conjunctive achievement goals over predicate fluents, not numeric fluents or numeric optimization.

These are relevant prior-work benchmarks but are not selected for the final three-class ASL-library evaluation. Some require distance, quantified, numeric, or program-synthesis semantics outside the current claim.

main_claim_requires: support_level is main_claim or planned_claim_target with strict ASL-library validation
selected external or feature-policy evidence is safely bound and selected
generated ASL has no grounded plan heads and no synthetic achievement names
not_counted_as_main_claim: planned target domains without current strict-profile ASL validation
local mechanism-stress domains that are not standard IPC benchmarks
boundary fragments requiring unsupported reachability, numeric, resource-instrument, or object-specific feature semantics
per-problem planner traces and raw qualitative policies without ASL compilation

Chen2026Moose.title: Satisficing and Optimal Generalised Planning via Goal Regression
Chen2026Moose.role: Defines TGI, SGI, OGI and uses ESHO domains such as Barman, Ferry, Gripper, Logistics, Miconic, Rovers, Satellite, and Transport, with path-finding components removed from some domains.

Drexler2022LearningSketches.title: Learning Sketches for Decomposing Planning Problems into Subproblems of Bounded Width
Drexler2022LearningSketches.role: Learns sketches with ASP over IPC-style domains such as Blocks-clear, Blocks-on, Childsnack, Delivery, Gripper, Miconic, Reward, Spanner, and Visitall.

Drexler2024SubgoalSketches.title: Expressing and Exploiting Subgoal Structure in Classical Planning Using Sketches
Drexler2024SubgoalSketches.role: Uses handcrafted sketches for IPC domains with provable well-formedness and small sketch width, including Barman, Childsnack, Driverlog, Floortile, Grid variants, Schedule, and Traveling Purchaser Problem.

Bonet2024GeneralPoliciesWidth.title: General Policies, Subgoal Structure, and Planning Width
Bonet2024GeneralPoliciesWidth.role: Provides the planning-width and sketch-theoretic basis for bounded serialized subgoal structure.

Bonet2024PolicyReuse.title: On Policy Reuse: An Expressive Language for Representing and Executing General Policies that Call Other Policies
Bonet2024PolicyReuse.role: Motivates reusable policy modules and calls, the closest prior representation to predicate-goal ASL modules.

Frances2021LearningGeneralPolicies.title: Learning General Planning Policies from Small Examples Without Supervision
Frances2021LearningGeneralPolicies.role: Learns feature-definable policies for Qclear, Qon, full Blocksworld, Gripper, Spanner, Miconic, Visitall, and Delivery; provides the strongest Blocksworld support precedent.

Yang2022PG3.title: PG3: Policy-Guided Planning for Generalized Policy Generation
Yang2022PG3.role: Uses six PDDL domains: Forest, Delivery, Miconic, Gripper, Ferry, and Spanner, and evaluates learned lifted decision-list policies directly on held-out problems.

SegoviaAguas2022BFGPpp.title: Representation and Synthesis of C++ Programs for Generalized Planning
SegoviaAguas2022BFGPpp.role: Uses numeric and STRIPS domains including Blocks, Corridor, Floyd, Gripper, Spanner, and Visitall to evaluate terminating program synthesis.

benchmark_source.name: potassco/pddl-instances
benchmark_source.local_snapshot_policy: Complete IPC PDDL directories are tracked under src/domains/<domain> with domain.pddl, train, test, and source.json. The train split uses floor(2/3 * instance_count); the remainder is held out for goal specification.
